metadata.name: Influxdb
metadata.description: Open-source time series database designed for high-performance storage and retrieval of time-stamped data. Commonly used for monitoring, analytics, and IoT applications.
metadata.guide:
## Overview
Open-source time series database designed for high-performance storage and retrieval of time-stamped data. Commonly used for monitoring, analytics, and IoT applications.

## How to Use
This template renders `compose.yaml` and `.env` as files.

1. Decide on service names, ports, volumes, credentials, and optional integrations before rendering the stack.
2. Fill in the required bootstrap org, bucket, and admin token values before first start.
3. Render the Compose project, review the generated files, and start it with Docker Compose in the target directory.

## Variable Guide
### Ports
- `ports_http`: Host port for HTTP API (8086); optional; default: 8086.

### Influxdb
- `influxdb_init_username`: Username for the initial admin; required; default: admin.
- `influxdb_init_password`: Password for the initial admin; required.
- `influxdb_init_org`: Organization created during the initial setup; required; default: homelab.
- `influxdb_init_bucket`: Bucket created during the initial setup; required; default: default.
- `influxdb_init_admin_token`: Admin API token created during the initial setup; required.

### Traefik
- `traefik_enabled`: Enable Traefik integration; optional; default: false.
- `traefik_host`: Hostname used for Traefik; optional.
- `traefik_domain`: Domain used for Traefik; required.
- `traefik_network`: Network used for Traefik; required; default: traefik.
- `traefik_tls_enabled`: Enable Traefik TLS; optional; default: false.
- `traefik_tls_certresolver`: Traefik Tls Certresolver; required; default: cloudflare; shown when: traefik_tls_enabled=true.

### General
- `service_name`: Name of the service; optional; default: influxdb.
- `container_timezone`: Timezone used inside the container; optional.
- `restart_policy`: Policy used for Restart; required; default: unless-stopped; options: unless-stopped, always, on-failure, no.

## References
- [InfluxDB](https://www.influxdata.com/)
- [Documentation](https://docs.influxdata.com/influxdb/v2/)
- [GitHub](https://github.com/influxdata/influxdb)

## Next Steps
Log in with your initial admin user:
```bash
Username: << influxdb_init_username >>
Password: << influxdb_init_password >>
Organization: << influxdb_init_org >>
Bucket: << influxdb_init_bucket >>
```
